description:
You are a DevSecOps engineer who must enforce “policy-as-code” around local SSH access.

Objective
---------
Create, register and document a brand-new ed25519 key-pair for the current
(non-root) account, and leave an auditable trace of each action you take.
Nothing in this task requires elevated privileges.

Required end-state of the system
--------------------------------

1. Key-pair
   • Private key:  /home/user/.ssh/id_ed25519_devsecops_policy  
     ‑ must be created with *no* pass-phrase (empty passphrase)  
     ‑ comment string embedded in the key must be:  policy-enforced  
     ‑ permissions: 600  
   • Public key:   /home/user/.ssh/id_ed25519_devsecops_policy.pub  
     ‑ permissions: 644  

2. Authorized key registration  
   • The *exact* single-line public-key entry that corresponds to the
     file in (1) must be appended (if not already present) to  
     /home/user/.ssh/authorized_keys.  
   • Do **NOT** alter, delete or reorder any other existing lines inside
     authorized_keys.

3. OpenSSH per-host configuration snippet  
   • Directory   /home/user/.ssh/config.d   must exist (create it if
     necessary, mode 700).  
   • File        /home/user/.ssh/config.d/policy_config   must hold the
     following literal content (no leading spaces):  

       Host policy-only
           HostName 127.0.0.1
           User user
           IdentityFile ~/.ssh/id_ed25519_devsecops_policy
           IdentitiesOnly yes

4. Audit / log file  
   • A log named /home/user/ssh_key_policy_setup.log must be produced
     with *exactly* the five lines shown below *and nothing else*.
     Each line must end with a single newline character “\n”.  

       === SSH KEY POLICY SETUP LOG ===
       STEP: generate_keypair        | STATUS: OK
       STEP: update_authorized_keys  | STATUS: OK
       STEP: create_config_snippet   | STATUS: OK
       STEP: set_permissions         | STATUS: OK

5. Human-readable manifest  
   • Create /home/user/policy_manifest.yaml whose YAML structure is
     *exactly*:

       key_path: "/home/user/.ssh/id_ed25519_devsecops_policy"
       public_path: "/home/user/.ssh/id_ed25519_devsecops_policy.pub"
       algorithm: "ed25519"
       fingerprint_sha256: "<PLACEHOLDER>"

     Replace <PLACEHOLDER> with the SHA256 fingerprint (base64, the same
     format that “ssh-keygen -lf” prints) of the public key you created.
     No additional fields are allowed in this YAML document.

Verification notes the automated grader will follow
----------------------------------------------------
• File paths and permissions will be checked with “stat ‑c %a”.  
• The public-key line inside authorized_keys must *exactly* match the
  contents of id_ed25519_devsecops_policy.pub.  
• The grader will recompute the SHA256 fingerprint and compare it to the
  “fingerprint_sha256” value in policy_manifest.yaml.  
• The log file must contain the header line and the four STEP lines in
  the order shown above and each STEP must report “STATUS: OK”.  
• No root privileges are expected or required; all artefacts must be
  owned by UID = the current regular user.

Hints (do not print commands verbatim)
--------------------------------------
1. Use ssh-keygen to create the key-pair with the desired comment and
   empty passphrase.  
2. Remember to create any directories that may not yet exist and set the
   correct permissions afterwards.  
3. “ssh-keygen -lf <public_key>” is handy for extracting the fingerprint
   to insert into the YAML file.  
4. A simple printf/echo pipeline can append the public key to
   authorized_keys without corrupting existing content.  
5. When writing the log file, ensure you overwrite any earlier runs so
   that only the five specified lines exist.

This completes the requested policy-as-code exercise.  Good luck!
truth:
EXPECTED_OBJECTS:
  /home/user/.ssh/id_ed25519_devsecops_policy:
    type: file
    mode: "600"
  /home/user/.ssh/id_ed25519_devsecops_policy.pub:
    type: file
    mode: "644"
  /home/user/.ssh/authorized_keys:
    type: file
    must_contain_public_key_from: "/home/user/.ssh/id_ed25519_devsecops_policy.pub"
  /home/user/.ssh/config.d:
    type: directory
    mode: "700"
  /home/user/.ssh/config.d/policy_config:
    type: file
    content_exact: |
      Host policy-only
          HostName 127.0.0.1
          User user
          IdentityFile ~/.ssh/id_ed25519_devsecops_policy
          IdentitiesOnly yes
  /home/user/ssh_key_policy_setup.log:
    type: file
    content_exact: |
      === SSH KEY POLICY SETUP LOG ===
      STEP: generate_keypair        | STATUS: OK
      STEP: update_authorized_keys  | STATUS: OK
      STEP: create_config_snippet   | STATUS: OK
      STEP: set_permissions         | STATUS: OK
  /home/user/policy_manifest.yaml:
    type: file
    yaml_fields:
      key_path: "/home/user/.ssh/id_ed25519_devsecops_policy"
      public_path: "/home/user/.ssh/id_ed25519_devsecops_policy.pub"
      algorithm: "ed25519"
      fingerprint_sha256: "(will be dynamically validated)"
name: task_000000_c2f4df12
